name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday, installment, and title loans at 1,400+ U.S. locations, with same-day cash available in-store or online.
description_long:
Advance America is one of the largest consumer lending chains in the United States, operating physical storefronts alongside an online application platform. The Jacksonville location at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave. serves the Commonwealth Village neighborhood, positioned between I-95 and I-295 west of downtown. The company describes itself as nationally recognized and fully accredited, with over 200,000 Google reviews and a 4.9-out-of-5 aggregate rating drawn from more than 125,000 customer reviews.

The store offers three core loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are structured as two-to-four-week short-term loans intended to bridge gaps between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment across multiple scheduled payments over a longer term, making them a step up in flexibility from payday loans. Title Loans are offered in the range of $2,000 to $25,000 through a partnership with LoanCenter, using the borrower's paid-off vehicle as collateral. The store also provides Western Union money transfer services. Applications can be submitted online, by phone for pre-qualification, or in person.

Several features differentiate this location from smaller independent lenders. Spanish-language service is explicitly available, with bilingual staff on-site. The store maintains consistent weekday hours (10am–6pm Mon–Thu, 9am–6pm Fri) and Saturday hours (9am–4pm), offering walk-in access six days a week. Requirements are straightforward: a government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN. A referral rewards program is also in place for existing customers.

Advance America fills a real need for consumers who lack access to traditional credit and need fast cash, but prospective borrowers should understand the trade-offs. Payday loans at this tier typically carry triple-digit APRs, and the two-to-four-week repayment window can be difficult to meet, risking rollover fees. Title loans, while offering larger amounts, put the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession if the loan is not repaid. These products are best treated as last-resort, short-term tools rather than ongoing financing solutions.
address: 2150 S Tamiami Trl Unit 15, Port Charlotte, FL 33948

company_info.headquarters: 2150 S Tamiami Trl Unit 15, Port Charlotte, FL 33948

pros: 4.9-star rating aggregated from 125,385+ customer reviews, indicating strong service consistency
Three distinct loan types (payday, installment, title) available at one location, covering a range of borrowing needs
Spanish-speaking staff available in-store for bilingual customers
Applications accepted online, by phone pre-qualification, or in person — multiple access channels
Title loans up to $25,000 for vehicle owners needing larger emergency amounts
Western Union money transfers available in addition to loans
Referral rewards program for existing customers
cons: Payday loans capped at $500 with a two-to-four-week repayment window — high rollover risk if cash flow doesn't recover
Title loans are handled through a third-party partner (LoanCenter), not directly by Advance America
Vehicle must be fully paid off and title in borrower's name to qualify for title loans — collateral risk of repossession
Closed Sundays; limited to six-day walk-in access
No mention of APR ranges on the location page — borrowers must inquire directly before committing
best_for: Employed consumers needing $100–$500 to bridge a short gap before their next paycheck
Borrowers needing $1,000 or less with the ability to repay in multiple installments
Vehicle owners with a paid-off car who need $2,000–$25,000 quickly and have no other credit options
Spanish-speaking consumers in the Jacksonville area who prefer bilingual in-person service
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for consumers with steady income who need fast cash under $1,000 and cannot qualify for traditional bank credit or a credit union PAL. The primary caveat is cost: payday and installment loans at this tier carry high APRs, and title loans put the borrower's vehicle at risk — these products should be used sparingly and only when the borrower has a clear repayment path.
services: Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000, in partnership with LoanCenter)
Referral rewards program for existing customers

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
